pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'Save the file as 'refraction_lab.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/'. Confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh.

Task description explicitly requires: 'Two sliders named 'n1' and 'n2' representing refractive indices, ranging from at least 1.0 to 2.5.' Verified in setup_task.sh instructions and export_result.sh slider detection logic.

Snell's Law formula: n1*sin(θ1) = n2*sin(θ2). To calculate refraction angle θ2, the formula is θ2 = asin((n1/n2)*sin(θ1)). Both trigonometric functions are required. Verified via physics reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Snell's_law and https://www.physicsclassroom.com/class/refrn/Lesson-2/Snell-s-Law

Task description states 'ranging from at least 1.0 to 2.5'. Refractive index of 1.0 corresponds to vacuum/air (air ≈ 1.0003). Verified via physics reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Refractive_index shows vacuum = 1.0, air = 1.0003.

Task description states 'ranging from at least 1.0 to 2.5'. Diamond has refractive index ≈ 2.417. The value 2.5 is above diamond and provides reasonable upper bound for physics simulation. Verified via physics reference: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_refractive_indices shows diamond = 2.417.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating an interactive physics simulation of light refraction in GeoGebra that demonstrates Snell's Law (n1*sin(θ1) = n2*sin(θ2)). The simulation must include two sliders named 'n1' and 'n2' representing refractive indices ranging from 1.0 (vacuum/air) to 2.5 (exceeding diamond's 2.417). Both sine and arcsine functions are mathematically required to implement Snell's Law: sin for the incident angle and asin for calculating the refraction angle. The construction must handle Total Internal Reflection (TIR) when arcsin receives a value exceeding 1 (when n1*sin(θ1)/n2 > 1). The completed file must be saved as 'refraction_lab.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
